data.name: Flyout_HardPowerOff_InProgress
data.value: Sending hard power-off to the monitor. If it accepts, the screen will go dark - press the monitor's physical power button to bring it back.

data.name: Flyout_HardPowerOff_NoResponseDetail
data.value: Monitor did not respond to DDC/CI.

data.name: Flyout_HardPowerOff_Success
data.value: Hard power-off sent. Press the monitor's physical power button to power it back on.

data.name: Flyout_HardPowerOff_WarningText
data.value: Warning, this action will power off the selected display. This warning will not show again.

data.name: Flyout_MonitorIconToggle_MasterTooltip
data.value: Sync all displays to master brightness Ctrl+click: sync all displays to the brightest individual

data.name: Flyout_MonitorIconToggle_ReadDegradedTooltip
data.value: Display is failing to report. Writing brightness may still work. Ctrl+click to power it off.

data.name: Flyout_Update_Tooltip
data.value: A new version is available. Click to review and install.

data.name: SettingsWindow_ConfirmOverlay_DefaultMessage
data.value: This deletes all name overrides, custom order, per-monitor overrides, and the known-displays history. The Display order & overrides list will repopulate from currently-connected monitors.

data.name: Settings_About_CheckForUpdates_Description
data.value: Periodically ask GitHub if a newer release is available.

data.name: Settings_About_DDCCorruption_Description
data.value: If a monitor becomes unrecoverable, its slider will show with a warning triangle glyph. The monitor will have to be power cycled to restore DDC.

data.name: Settings_About_NightLightCorruption_Description
data.value: If Night Light becomes unresponsive, win+alt+shift+b then signing out and back in should clear it.

data.name: Settings_About_ShowUpdateButton_Description
data.value: Float an Update! button in the top-right of the flyout while a new version is available.

data.name: Settings_About_ShowUpdateNotifications_Description
data.value: Raise a tray notification when a new version is detected and the flyout isn't open.

data.name: Settings_About_Tagline
data.value: A tray-based brightness controller for DDC/CI monitors.

data.name: Settings_About_UpdateInterval_Description
data.value: How often the background update check runs. The timer resets every time you click Check for updates.

data.name: Settings_About_UpdateStatus_LastCheckedFormat
data.value: You're up to date. Last checked {0}.

data.name: Settings_About_UpdateStatus_NeverChecked
data.value: No update check has run yet.

data.name: Settings_Environmental_DisabledPeriodFollowTheSun_ToolTip
data.value: Reanchor the Start / End boundaries to twilight, sunrise, solar noon, and sunset so the disabled period drifts with the day/night cycle across the year. Sun events already honour the profile's Use daylight savings flag, so this does not compound with that shift.

data.name: Settings_Environmental_DisabledPeriod_ToolTip
data.value: Carve out a window inside the 24h cycle where the curves do not apply. Drag the white pins above the chart, or edit the Start / End boxes below, to set the boundaries. Start past End wraps midnight. Per-profile.

data.name: Settings_Environmental_FollowTheSun_ToolTip
data.value: Reanchor curve points to twilight, sunrise, solar noon, and sunset for the current profile. Each point keeps its proportional position within its sun-segment, so the curve drifts in clock-time as days lengthen and shorten across the year. Per-profile, applied destructively to the stored points - missed days catch up automatically.

data.name: Settings_Environmental_GeoLocation_Description
data.value: Decimal degrees. Positive values are north / east.

data.name: Settings_Environmental_OffsetMode_ToolTip
data.value: When on, curves apply additively/subtractively to the live brightness / night-light value. Drag the dashed lines to clamp how far the offset can swing.

data.name: Settings_Environmental_PreviewSweep_ToolTip
data.value: Animate the full day's curve over 10 seconds, applying values to monitors and night light as it sweeps. Requires at least one slider in the flyout to be set to curve mode.

data.name: Settings_Environmental_ResetCurves_ConfirmMessage
data.value: This resets the brightness and night-light curves for the current mode (offset or absolute) on the selected profile back to their flat defaults. This cannot be undone.

data.name: Settings_Environmental_ResetCurves_ToolTip
data.value: Reset the selected profile's brightness, night-light, and offset curves back to their flat defaults. Per-profile, applied immediately.

data.name: Settings_Environmental_SectionDescription
data.value: Drive brightness and night-light from the local sun position. Curves are stored per profile; geo coordinates are global.

data.name: Settings_Environmental_ShowCursorReadout_ToolTip
data.value: Show a vertical scrubber and per-curve value markers while the cursor is over the editor.

data.name: Settings_Environmental_ShowSunOverlay_ToolTip
data.value: Shade twilight (orange) and night (greyish blue) bands behind the curves using the geo coordinates below.

data.name: Settings_Environmental_UseDaylightSavings_ToolTip
data.value: When on, sun-position calculations follow daylight savings - sun events line up with wall-clock time across the year. When off, the timezone's standard offset is used year-round, so sun events stay anchored to standard time and the curve doesn't shift by an hour at DST boundaries. Per-profile.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_MasterSliderTracking_Description
data.value: How the master slider reflects the individual monitor sliders when it's not driving them.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_MouseWheelStep_Description
data.value: How many percent each mouse wheel notch adjusts a brightness slider in the flyout.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_NumberKeysSwitchProfile_Description
data.value: While the brightness flyout is focused, press 1-4 to switch to the matching profile.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_NumberKeysSwitchProfile_Title
data.value: Number keys switch profile in flyout

data.name: Settings_Flyout_PowerButtonOnlyEnabled_Description
data.value: When on, the footer power button only powers off monitors enabled in the app. When off, it powers off every monitor.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_PowerButtonOnlyEnabled_Title
data.value: Power button affects only enabled monitors

data.name: Settings_Flyout_PreserveSliderOffsets_Description
data.value: When the master slider pushes an individual monitor past 0% or 100%, retain the overflow so later master adjustments restore the original brightness differences between monitors.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_PreserveSliderOffsets_Title
data.value: Preserve slider offsets (prevent slider offset degeneration)

data.name: Settings_Flyout_RestoreUndockState_Description
data.value: When the app launches, restore the flyout's docked or undocked state from the previous session. When off, the flyout always opens docked.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_ShowDisplayNumberBadge_Description
data.value: Overlay the OS-assigned display number inside each monitor icon in the brightness flyout.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_ShowDisplayNumberBadge_Title
data.value: Show display number on monitor icons

data.name: Settings_Flyout_ShowDisplaySettingsButton_Description
data.value: Show the link to Windows display settings in the brightness flyout footer.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_ShowEnvironmentalCurvesButton_Description
data.value: Show the environmental curves toggle button in the brightness flyout footer.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_ShowIndividualSliders_Description
data.value: Show the per-monitor sliders in the brightness flyout.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_ShowMasterSlider_Description
data.value: Show the All Displays master slider in the brightness flyout.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_ShowMonitorPowerButtons_Description
data.value: Display a per-monitor power off button next to each monitor in the brightness flyout.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_ShowNightLightKelvinLabel_Description
data.value: Display the current color temperature (e.g. 4500K) above the night light slider in the brightness flyout.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_ShowNightLightKelvinLabel_Title
data.value: Show kelvin label on night light slider

data.name: Settings_Flyout_ShowPowerButton_Description
data.value: Show a power button in the brightness flyout footer.

data.name: Settings_Flyout_ShowUndockButton_Description
data.value: Show the undock button in the flyout. When off, the flyout always stays anchored to the tray.

data.name: Settings_General_ApplyBrightnessOnStartup_Description
data.value: Restore the last-saved brightness profile on launch.

data.name: Settings_General_Autosave_Description
data.value: Automatically save to the selected profile whenever a monitor setting changes.

data.name: Settings_General_EnvironmentalCurveTickInterval_Description
data.value: How often the brightness / night-light curves re-sample and apply. Lower = quicker reaction to fast-changing curves; higher = less background work. Identical values across ticks are filtered before any DDC write.

data.name: Settings_General_GammaRampNightLight_Description
data.value: Drive night light by writing the GPU's gamma LUT directly instead of going through Windows Night Light. Survives locked-down profiles and stuck BlueLightReduction services. Doesn't work on HDR displays.

data.name: Settings_General_InstallConfirm_Message_Format
data.value: This will copy the running exe to "{0}" and register it in Windows Settings > Apps.

data.name: Settings_General_InstallSystemWideConfirm_Message_Format
data.value: This will copy the running exe to "{0}" and register it in Windows Settings > Apps. Requires admin.

data.name: Settings_General_InstalledOutOfDate_Format
data.value: Installed build {0}, running build {1}. Click Update to overwrite.{2}

data.name: Settings_General_InvertNightLightSlider_Description
data.value: Reverse the night light slider so dragging left increases warmth and dragging right cools the screen.

data.name: Settings_General_NightLightPDBDownloadTimeout_Description
data.value: HTTP timeout the night-light fallback uses when downloading the SettingsHandlers_Display PDB from Microsoft's symbol server. Only matters after a Windows update changes the binary layout; raise this on slow or metered connections.

data.name: Settings_General_NotInstalled_Format
data.value: Not installed. Will be placed at "{0}".{1}

data.name: Settings_General_ProfileCard_DragTooltip
data.value: Drag or Ctrl+↑/↓ to reorder; double-click or type to rename

data.name: Settings_General_RearrangeProfiles_Description
data.value: Drag the cards to reorder them, then Apply to swap the profiles' saved data between slots.

data.name: Settings_General_RunOnStartup_Description
data.value: Launch BrightnessTrayAppDotNET when you sign in to Windows.

data.name: Settings_General_SettingsHandlerNightLight_Description
data.value: Route night-light writes through the same setting handler that the Windows Settings slider uses. Keeps the Settings UI's cached slider position in sync with what the flyout is showing. Recommended on builds where the BlueLightReduction service goes stale and the registry path stops being picked up.

data.name: Settings_General_ShowNightLightSlider_Description
data.value: Show the night light strength slider in the brightness flyout. When off, the slider row is hidden.

data.name: Settings_General_StoreNotInstalled
data.value: Not installed via the Windows Store.

data.name: Settings_General_StoreRunning
data.value: Currently running from a Windows Store package.

data.name: Settings_General_TurnOffNightLightAtZeroStrength_Description
data.value: When the night light slider hits 0, also disable night light. Toggling night light back on restores the last non-zero strength.

data.name: Settings_General_TurnOffNightLightAtZeroStrength_Title
data.value: Turn off night light at zero strength

data.name: Settings_General_UninstallIncomplete_Message
data.value: Uninstall completed with warnings. Some files or registry entries may need manual removal. Check the install location and Windows Settings > Apps.

data.name: Settings_Hotkeys_DecrementMasterBrightness_Description
data.value: Lower every monitor's brightness by one step (matches one tray-scroll notch).

data.name: Settings_Hotkeys_DecrementNightLight_Description
data.value: Lower night-light strength by one step (matches one tray-scroll notch).

data.name: Settings_Hotkeys_FullBright_Description
data.value: Set every monitor to 100%. Press again to restore the previous brightness.

data.name: Settings_Hotkeys_FullDim_Description
data.value: Set every monitor to 0%. Press again to restore the previous brightness.

data.name: Settings_Hotkeys_IncrementMasterBrightness_Description
data.value: Raise every monitor's brightness by one step (matches one tray-scroll notch).

data.name: Settings_Hotkeys_IncrementNightLight_Description
data.value: Raise night-light strength by one step (matches one tray-scroll notch).

data.name: Settings_Hotkeys_NormalizeBrightnesses_Description
data.value: Sync every monitor's brightness to the master slider's current value.

data.name: Settings_Hotkeys_OpenFlyout_Description
data.value: Show the brightness flyout above the tray icon.

data.name: Settings_Hotkeys_PowerOffAllMonitors_Description
data.value: Turn every monitor off (uses the configured power-off mode).

data.name: Settings_Hotkeys_PowerOffSpecificMonitor_Description
data.value: Turn off the monitor selected in the dropdown.

data.name: Settings_Hotkeys_SectionDescription
data.value: Bind a system-wide keyboard shortcut to each action. Bindings require at least one modifier key (Ctrl, Alt, Shift, or Win). All actions start unbound.

data.name: Settings_Hotkeys_ToggleNightLight_Description
data.value: Turn Windows night light on or off.

data.name: Settings_Monitors_BrightnessRate_Description
data.value: How long a brightness value must be set before the app will try to apply it.

data.name: Settings_Monitors_ClearDisplays_ConfirmMessage
data.value: This deletes all name overrides, custom order, per-monitor overrides, and the known-displays history. The Display order & overrides list will repopulate from currently-connected monitors.

data.name: Settings_Monitors_DDCOperationTimeout_Description
data.value: Maximum time any single DDC call is allowed to block before the app moves on. Defends against driver-layer hangs piling up threads or stalling shutdown. Lower = faster failure for stuck monitors; higher = more tolerant of slow-responding panels.

data.name: Settings_Monitors_DefaultSort_Description
data.value: Ordering used for displays that haven't been manually reordered. Applies on hot-plug.

data.name: Settings_Monitors_DisplayOrder_Description
data.value: Rename, reorder, and set per-monitor DDC/CI overrides.

data.name: Settings_Monitors_IdentityStrategy_Description
data.value: Which attribute keys profiles, names, and ordering to each physical monitor. Changing this re-enumerates and may orphan saved profile entries.

data.name: Settings_Monitors_PowerOffMode_Description
data.value: How the 'power off' action actually powers a monitor off. Sleep = standby (VCP 0x02), Soft = DPM off (0x04), Hard = write-only off (0x05). Some monitors ignore Soft; Hard will most likely require physical input to wake.

data.name: Settings_Monitors_ValidationAttempts_Description
data.value: Number of times to retry a brightness read before giving up. Each retry waits one validation dwell, and the final attempt also refreshes the cached HMONITOR. Higher = more tolerant of transient I2C noise; lower = faster failure for genuinely stuck monitors.

data.name: Settings_Monitors_ValidationDwell_Description
data.value: How long to wait after a settled brightness write before reading it back to confirm it applied. Increase on slow-responding monitors.

data.name: Settings_Theme_BackgroundColor_Description
data.value: Override the default background color. Each variant falls back to the default when unset.

data.name: Settings_Theme_BrightColor_Description
data.value: Color used at 100% brightness. Blends toward the dim color as brightness decreases. Each variant falls back to the default when unset.

data.name: Settings_Theme_BrightnessCurveColor_Description
data.value: Stroke color for the brightness curve and its legend swatch. Each variant falls back to the default when unset.

data.name: Settings_Theme_CurrentTimeMarkerColor_Description
data.value: Color of the dashed line marking the current time on the curve editor. Each variant falls back to the default when unset.

data.name: Settings_Theme_DimColor_Description
data.value: Color used at 0% brightness. Blends toward the bright color as brightness increases. Each variant falls back to the default when unset.

data.name: Settings_Theme_DynamicIconTracking_Description
data.value: How the dynamic tray icon reflects the individual monitor brightness values.

data.name: Settings_Theme_FooterBackgroundColor_Description
data.value: Override the flyout footer background color. Each variant falls back to the default when unset.

data.name: Settings_Theme_FontSize_Description
data.value: Controls the overall size of the tray context menu. All elements scale from font size.

data.name: Settings_Theme_GridLineColor_Description
data.value: Color of the horizontal and vertical gridlines drawn behind the curves. Each variant falls back to the default when unset.

data.name: Settings_Theme_NightBackdropColor_Description
data.value: Translucent band shaded behind the curves during astronomical night. Each variant falls back to the default when unset.

data.name: Settings_Theme_NightLightCurveColor_Description
data.value: Stroke color for the night-light curve and its legend swatch. Each variant falls back to the default when unset.

data.name: Settings_Theme_RoundedCorners_Description
data.value: Applies rounded corners to the flyout, settings cards, buttons, and menus. Turn off for a squared-off look.

data.name: Settings_Theme_SliderIndicator_Description
data.value: The glyph drawn as the brightness-slider thumb. Each option keeps its own font family and size.

data.name: Settings_Theme_StaticIconColor_Description
data.value: Override the static tray icon color. Each variant falls back to the default when unset.

data.name: Settings_Theme_TextColor_Description
data.value: Override the default text color. Each variant falls back to the default when unset.

data.name: Settings_Theme_ThemeStyle_Description
data.value: Follow Windows, or force light / dark mode for this app.

data.name: Settings_Theme_TrackEnabledOnly_Description
data.value: Exclude disabled monitors from the dynamic icon calculation. When all monitors are disabled, the icon shows full brightness.

data.name: Settings_Theme_TrayIconStyle_Description
data.value: Dynamic eclipses with brightness. Static locks the icon at 50% eclipse.

data.name: Settings_Theme_TwilightBackdropColor_Description
data.value: Translucent band shaded behind the curves between sunrise/sunset and astronomical twilight. Each variant falls back to the default when unset.

data.name: Settings_TrayIcon_MenuPosition_Description
data.value: Classic opens at the cursor. Modern docks the menu above the taskbar like the Windows 11 system flyouts.

data.name: Settings_TrayIcon_ModifiedActions_Description
data.value: Assign actions to modified clicks or scrolls on the tray icon. Entries set to Nothing fall back to the default behavior.

data.name: Settings_TrayIcon_MouseWheel_Description
data.value: Enable scrolling the mouse wheel while hovering the tray icon. Configure targets below in 'Modified actions'. Does *not* use an always-active global mouse hook.

data.name: Settings_TrayIcon_ShowAllDisplaysPowerSelector_Description
data.value: Display a single entry that powers every display off.

data.name: Settings_TrayIcon_ShowIndividualPowerSelectors_Description
data.value: Display per-monitor power toggles in the tray right-click menu.

data.name: Settings_TrayIcon_ShowProfileSelectors_Description
data.value: Display brightness profile entries in the tray right-click menu.

data.name: Uninstaller_DefaultDescription
data.value: This will remove the installed copy of BrightnessTrayAppDotNET and its registration in Windows Apps. Choose what to do with your settings.

data.name: Uninstaller_Description_Format
data.value: This will remove BrightnessTrayAppDotNET installed at "{0}" and its entry in Windows Settings > Apps. Choose what to do with your settings.

data.name: Uninstaller_KeepSettings_Description
data.value: Leave settings.xml in place so a future install picks them up.

data.name: UpdateDialog_DefaultDescription
data.value: A new version is available. The app will restart after installing.

data.name: UpdateDialog_NoChangelog
data.value: No release notes were published for this version.

data.name: UpdateNotification_BodyFormat
data.value: A new release ({0}) is available. Open the flyout to review and install.

data.name: Settings_General_RenderingBackend_Description
data.value: "GPU preferred" uses Avalonia's Windows GPU path with software fallback. "Software" forces CPU rendering. GPU rendering is faster but has more RAM overhead. Restart required.
